extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won stage 10 from the breakaway with a bike throw, while POGAČAR attacked in the final meters to retain yellow by 11 seconds over KÄMNA.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR visibly accelerated in final 100m specifically to defend yellow jersey time gaps before mountain stages.
SCHULTZ Nick nearly won despite Trek Breakaway inexperience, showing solo TT strength on gradual finishing climb.
KÄMNA Lennard rode to within 11 seconds of yellow without team support, signal of independent GC threat.

rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: second Tour stage win via perfectly-timed bike throw against fresher climbers on uphill finish
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: retained yellow despite weakened team, actively chased time gaps rather than gift jersey pre-mountains
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: rose to virtual yellow without BENNETT support, 8:45 gap closed to 11 seconds shows independent climbing form
rider_signals.SCHULTZ Nick: positive: nearly won first Tour stage via breakaway, lost only to bike throw against experienced finisher
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: gained 28 seconds on POGAČAR without attacking, now 39 seconds back with mountains approaching
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: highest-ranked American at 11th GC, maintained position through transition stage
